threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for a 'plastic bag' at the market instead of just saying 'bag'. The vendors give you that look like you’ve just landed from Mars.
Last weekend, saw a guy in a full tux singing opera to the night bus passengers. His voice was surprisingly good but the bus driver looked furious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler chasing pigeons while their dad argued with the kebab shop owner over extra spicy sauce. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you see a techno party start at noon on a Tuesday. Who needs sleep when you have Berghain vibes at brunch?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you can spot a tourist by the way they flinch at the sirens. We just shrug and keep walking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched two guys in suits debating Muni’s delays while a pigeon casually helped itself to a dropped burrito in the middle of the street.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just tapas bars—they miss the local shortcuts through the park that lead to the real heart of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewildered expressions when the trams are late; locals just shrug and check their phones.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when the 38 bus comes screeching to a halt two inches from the curb, while seasoned locals just step aside like it's nothing.
Last night on the 45, a guy tried to sell everyone homemade cocktails from a backpack. Smelled like a proper pub; passengers were torn between curiosity and disbelief.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler argue in Turkish with their mom over the last Stück Kuchen at the local bakery. Pure Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar where they serve homemade pickles alongside craft beer. It's a uniquely Berlin twist that surprises every time.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to stock up on snacks before a BART closure, while the newbies just stand confused at the Civic Center exit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo waiting for a bus on Eddy, smooth jazz playing from his portable speaker. Only in the TL, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all about the Gaudí façades, but the real charm is in the hidden squares where locals sip café amb llet, not crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewilderment when the tram doors don’t open automatically—every local knows to press the button.
